Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order As To Confidential Information; Declaration Of Jonathan D. Sayre; Declaration Of Peter Sauerwein; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Feb-23-2023 Continued To Mar-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEVEN J. BRADY, Esq. of Brady Law Group (Tel: 415-459-7300) for plaintiff;
Atty. JONATHON D. SAYRE, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (Tel: 949-440-6690) for defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order As To Confidential Information.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Argument presented by counsel for both sides.

The Court continues this motion for further hearing to occur at a time after defendant has provided supplemental responses to the plaintiff regarding plaintiff's motion to compel.

The matter is continued to March 30, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery Calendar to be heard by Judge Pro Tem William Lynn.

A supplemental brief as to this motion may be filed by the defendant no later than March 22, 2023 and a supplemental brief may be filed by the plaintiff no later than March 25, 2023.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Thereunder; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-23-2023 Continued To Mar-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEVEN J. BRADY, Esq. of Brady Law Group (Tel: 415-459-7300) for plaintiff;
Atty. JONATHON D. SAYRE, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (Tel: 949-440-6690) for defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WAYNE PHILLIPS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Thereunder; Request For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Argument presented by counsel for both sides.

The Court rules as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted. Defendant is to provide supplemental responses that are code-compliant and without any new objections by March 17, 2023. For any withheld documents, defendant is to provide a code-compliant privilege log.

As to the request for sanctions, the Court continues this matter for further hearing to March 30, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery Calendar to be heard by Judge Pro Tem William Lynn.

Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel and to the pro tem judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-01-2022 Set For Trial On Jun-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
